heading: Cockburn Central vs Codjatotine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cockburn Central, Western Australia and Codjatotine, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cockburn Central has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Codjatotine (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cockburn Central or Codjatotine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cockburn Central has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Codjatotine scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cockburn Central scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Codjatotine scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cockburn Central or Codjatotine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cockburn Central has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
